title: Stock Market News, June 30, 2026: Nasdaq Advances, Climbs 21% for Quarter
summary: Stock Market News, June 30, 2026: Nasdaq Advances, Climbs 21% for Quarter
extracted_text: S&P 500 rose 15% for Q2, Nasdaq jumped 21% — biggest quarterly rallies since Q2 2020. Dow gained 13% — best quarter since Q4 2022. Asian markets mixed with Nikkei 70,426.72 (+0.52%), Hang Seng 22,881.02 (-0.63%), Shanghai 4,100.20 (+0.14%). Yen hovers near 40-year low. Oil fell on US-Iran ceasefire talks. Treasury yields hit one-week high.

title: Stock market news for June 30, 2026 — Record Closes & Strongest Half in Years
summary: Stock market news for June 30, 2026 — Record Closes & Strongest Half in Years
extracted_text: Dow closed at record 52,319.20 (+136.46 pts, +0.26%). S&P 500 7,499.36 (+0.79%). Nasdaq 26,213.72 (+1.52%). Driven by semiconductor rally: SMH +3%, NVDA +2.6%, AMD +7.7%, INTC +6%. H1 2026: Dow +8.9%, S&P +9.6%, Nasdaq +12%+, Russell 2000 +22% (best H1 since 1991). Q2: S&P +14.9%, Nasdaq +21.4%, Dow +12.9%. Consumer confidence 91.2 vs 94.2 est. JOLTS 7.59M vs 7.3M forecast. Key analyst: FBIN upgraded to Buy, GS/MS downgraded to Underperform.

title: U.S. indexes wrap best quarter since 2020 — tech rally, Middle East peace hopes
summary: U.S. indexes wrap best quarter since 2020 — tech rally, Middle East peace hopes
extracted_text: Dow +0.26% at 52,319.20 (second record close). S&P 500 +0.79% at 7,499.36. Nasdaq +1.52% at 26,213.72. Russell 2000 +0.46% at 3,024.37 (best YTD at +21.86%). VIX -6.8% to 16.45. 10Y Treasury +3.9 bps to 4.461%. Brent crude +0.67% above $73. Supreme Court 5-4 ruling rejected Trump attempt to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook — protects Fed independence. US-Iran ceasefire talks in Qatar (Witkoff, no direct talks). Bitcoin -1.4% to $59,030, Ethereum below $1.6K, MSTR -7.2% proposing $1.25B BTC sale.

title: Nike (NKE) Q4 2026 earnings: EPS $0.20 beats, China down 12%, stock volatile
summary: Nike (NKE) Q4 2026 earnings: EPS $0.20 beats, China down 12%, stock volatile
extracted_text: Adjusted EPS $0.20 vs $0.13 consensus (beat). GAAP EPS $0.72 including $986M IEEPA tariff refund. Revenue $10.97B vs $10.86B est (-1% YoY from $11.10B). North America $4.83B (+3%, missed $4.88B est). Greater China $1.30B (-12%, beat $1.24B est). Gross margin improved 890 bps to 49.2%. CEO Elliott Hill: 'Results aren't there yet' — Jordan/Sportswear negative growth expected through most of FY2027. CFO Matt Friend departing Aug 17, replaced by Pfizer exec David Denton. Investor Day Nov 16-17, 2026. Stock initially fell 8% AH, recovered to flat/slightly negative.

title: Asian markets mixed as yen hovers near four-decade lows, US jobs data in focus
summary: Asian markets mixed as yen hovers near four-decade lows, US jobs data in focus
extracted_text: Asian stocks were mixed as investors nervously awaited key US jobs data that could help determine Federal Reserve policy path. Japanese yen hovering near four-decade lows, traders on high alert for potential currency intervention. Nikkei 225 +0.52% at 70,426.72. Hang Seng -0.63% at 22,881.02. Shanghai Composite +0.14% at 4,100.20. Kospi -1.81%. ASX 200 -0.64%. BSE Sensex +0.62%.

title: ADP employment, manufacturing PMI, and oil inventories due Wednesday July 1
summary: ADP employment, manufacturing PMI, and oil inventories due Wednesday July 1
extracted_text: ADP Employment Change for June expected at +120,000 vs prior +122,000 at 8:15 AM ET. ISM Manufacturing PMI for June expected at 53.9-54.0 vs prior 53.6 at 10:00 AM ET. ISM Manufacturing Prices and Employment also due. Construction Spending at 10:00 AM. EIA weekly crude oil inventories at 10:30 AM. Challenger Job Cuts also scheduled.

title: Supreme Court Protects Fed Independence — Rejects Trump Bid to Fire Governor Cook
summary: Supreme Court Protects Fed Independence — Rejects Trump Bid to Fire Governor Cook
extracted_text: In a historic 5-4 ruling, the Supreme Court rejected President Trump's attempt to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook over unproven mortgage fraud allegations. The majority faulted Trump for not giving Cook notice and a chance to be heard. The decision ensures Fed governors cannot be fired by the president 'for cause' without proof of wrongdoing, reinforcing central bank independence. Contrasts with a separate court ruling on executive branch firing powers. Fed has held rates at 3.50-3.75% for four consecutive meetings under Chair Kevin Warsh.

title: US Treasury Yield Curve: 10Y 4.38%, 2Y 4.14%, 2s10s spread +28bps
extracted_text: The 10-year Treasury yield is 4.38%, the 2-year is 4.14%, and the 30-year is 4.86%, as of June 30, 2026. The 2s10s spread is +28 basis points. FOMC held rates at 3.50-3.75% at June meeting. FOMC minutes from April 28-29 showed median modal path of two 25bp cuts starting Q3/Q4 2026. Market-implied probability of rate hike by Q1 2027 at ~30%. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. Next FOMC: July 28-29, 2026.

title: DXY at 101.37, Yen near 40-year low; Gold under pressure at ~$3,978
summary: DXY at 101.37, Yen near 40-year low; Gold under pressure at ~$3,978
extracted_text: DXY rose to 101.3696 on July 1, +0.18% from prior session, +2.17% over past month, +4.75% YoY. Yen near 40-year low vs USD, intervention may hinge on US jobs data (Commonwealth Bank). Gold (XAU/USD) at $3,978 under pressure from stronger USD and Fed tightening expectations. WTI futures $70.91 (+0.23%), Brent $73.43 (+0.65%). Oil prices slipped on Tuesday with US-Iran ceasefire talks in Qatar.

title: AeroVironment +30% on earnings; Concentrix -22%; Goldman/MS downgraded
summary: AeroVironment +30% on earnings; Concentrix -22%; Goldman/MS downgraded
extracted_text: AVAV +30%: EPS $1.84 vs $1.46 est, revenue $642M vs $559M est. CNXC -22% AH: EPS $2.63 vs $2.64 est, revenue $2.46B vs $2.47B, lower forward guidance. GS & MS downgraded to Underperform (Oppenheimer): 'take the money and run' — recommends Ares, BX, KKR instead. FBIN upgraded to Buy (Truist, PT $70 from $45). UNH PT raised to $468 (Morgan Stanley). HON initiated Outperform PT $255 (Daiwa). TSLA Q2 deliveries ~10K above est per Deutsche Bank, led by Europe strength. LOGI downgraded to Underperform PT $86 (BofA) on memory chip price hikes hitting demand. DLR -4.5% on $7.8B data center purchase from Blackstone. Kering -5% ahead of Q2 results; luxury peers LVMH, Richemont, Hermes all down 1-2%.

title: European markets open soft: DAX -0.18%, FTSE -0.23%, CAC -0.21%
summary: European markets open soft: DAX -0.18%, FTSE -0.23%, CAC -0.21%
extracted_text: DAX at 24,626.89 (-0.18%), FTSE 100 at 10,484.22 (-0.23%), CAC 40 at 8,367.33 (-0.21%), Euro Stoxx 50 at 6,231.63 (+0.16%). European markets showing soft open following mixed Asian session. STOXX 600 advanced 1.6% in June and is up ~9% for Q2, marking its strongest quarterly performance since October 2020, as oil prices moved back to pre-war levels. EU imposed 50% duty on steel imports above quota effective July 1.

title: Elon Musk reclaims $1 trillion net worth; Apple supply chain leak; Central banks plan to cut USD allocations
summary: Elon Musk reclaims $1 trillion net worth; Apple supply chain leak; Central banks plan to cut USD allocations
extracted_text: Elon Musk net worth back above $1T after Tesla (+8.6%) and SpaceX (+7.6%) rallies. Holds ~700M TSLA shares and 4.8B SpaceX shares. Apple supply chain leak: ransomware group stole iPhone 18 Pro component lists from Tata Electronics. OMFIF survey of central banks managing $10T: for first time, more plan to reduce USD allocations than increase over next decade, citing political risks. SpaceX (SPCX) added to Nasdaq-100 (expected $4.3B passive inflows) and Russell 1000.

title: Yahoo Finance Pre-Market Futures: S&P -0.32%, Dow -0.30%, Nasdaq -0.53%
summary: Yahoo Finance Pre-Market Futures: S&P -0.32%, Dow -0.30%, Nasdaq -0.53%
extracted_text: S&P Futures 7,524.00 (-24.25, -0.32%), Dow Futures 52,514.00 (-156.00, -0.30%), Nasdaq Futures 30,362.25 (-161.25, -0.53%), Russell 2000 Futures 3,034.50. US markets open in 3.5 hours. After best quarter since 2020, futures indicating modest pullback ahead of ADP and ISM data.

title: Rocket Lab soars on acquisition; Alcoa to acquire South32 aluminum assets for up to $5.6B
summary: Rocket Lab soars on acquisition; Alcoa to acquire South32 aluminum assets for up to $5.6B
extracted_text: Tech and industrial stocks led advances on June 30. Rocket Lab soared on acquisition. Alcoa to acquire South32 aluminum assets for up to $5.6B in cash-and-stock deal covering operations in Australia, Brazil, and South Africa. Ipsen to buy Swiss biotech Memo in deal exceeding $800M (second acquisition this week after Kartos Therapeutics). Galderma's Botox rival rejected again by FDA. EU limits tariff-free steel imports, imposes 50% duty above quota effective July 1.
